response.justification: The candidate is a strong individual contributor with ~15 years of experience and genuinely relevant developer-tooling work (test runners like Trial, Fluent-Asserts, CI/CD and GitLab setup, MCP code generators). However, this is a people-leadership role (Level II Manager) with hard minimums of 2 years people management and 3 years technical leadership, and the resume shows no formal management, no team leadership, and no evidence of running one-on-ones, setting team priorities, or owning performance/roadmap. The mobile-ecosystem exposure is also thin (some iOS/Apple TV work years ago). Technically capable, but the core management requirements are unmet.
strengths: Extensive developer-tooling and testing-infrastructure experience (test runners, assertion libs, CI/CD)
Long, broad engineering background across web, mobile, embedded, and microservices
Strong knowledge-sharing track record (workshops, courses, talks) suggesting mentoring ability
concerns: No demonstrated people-management or formal team-leadership experience (a hard minimum)
All roles are individual-contributor titles; no evidence of roadmap ownership or performance management
Limited and dated mobile-platform ecosystem depth relative to the role's focus
